# Repository Guidelines

## Context Discipline

- Keep relevant technical and operational context in repository files (track docs, handoff notes, revisit logs, and module-level `AGENTS.md` files).
- Update context files when reality changes, assumptions are invalidated, or new constraints are discovered.
- Prefer small, dated updates in files over implicit context in chat history.
- Keep context next to the code it describes. For example, testing/runtime guidance belongs in `tests/README.md` and `helpers/README.md`, not in a generic top-level `docs/` page.

## Project Structure & Module Organization

- App code: `src/app/**`.
- Server runtime and APIs: `src/server/**`.
- Data layer: `src/db/**`.
- Shared contracts/types/utilities: `src/shared/**` and `src/types/**`.
- Tests: unit tests as `*.spec.ts` in `src/**`; Playwright tests in `tests/**`; legacy reference in `e2e/**`.
- Assets/public: `public/`; theming in `src/styles.scss` and `_theme-colors.scss`.

Start with the nearest applicable module guidance:

- `src/app/AGENTS.md`
- `src/server/AGENTS.md`
- `src/db/AGENTS.md`
- `tests/AGENTS.md`

More specific guidance also exists deeper in some subtrees (for example `src/server/config/AGENTS.md`).

## Build, Test, and Development Commands

- `bun run dev:start` — run Angular dev server at `http://localhost:4200`.
- `bun run build:app` — build client + server bundles.
- `bun run test:unit` — run unit tests.
- `bun run test:e2e` — run Playwright e2e.
- `bun run lint:fix` / `bun run lint:check` — lint with autofix and verification.
- `bun run format:write` — format with Prettier.
- `bun run docker:start` / `bun run docker:stop` — start/stop local services.
- Database: `bun run db:migrate`, `bun run db:push`, `bun run db:setup`, `bun run db:reset`.

## Type Safety (Always Full Types)

- End-to-end types are mandatory.
- Prefer inferred/derived types from Drizzle schema and Effect schema outputs.
- Prefer TypeScript return type inference for functions. Add an explicit return type only when it is genuinely needed for clarity, API boundaries, overloads, recursion, or to prevent an incorrect inferred type from escaping.
- Avoid `any`, unchecked `as`, and `unknown` without narrowing.
- Do not use `unknown as ...` (or similar cast bypasses) to force client types to compile; fix the source type contract/runtime mismatch instead.

## Error Handling Discipline

- Never hide defects by swallowing errors.
- If an error is expected and recoverable, map it explicitly to a typed/domain outcome.
- If an error is unexpected, fail loudly and keep enough context to debug root cause.
- Prefer preventing repeated failures with follow-up fixes/tests over adding silent fallbacks.
- RPC and Effect error channels use `Schema.TaggedError` end to end; do not introduce string-literal business errors in contracts or handlers.
- Keep defects as defects until the HTTP/RPC boundary. Only expected recoverable failures belong in typed error unions.
- When an external unknown error must cross a typed boundary, wrap it in a tagged error field using `Schema.Defect`.

## Conventions

- TypeScript strict mode is enforced.
- Indentation: 2 spaces; filenames `kebab-case.ts`; symbols `camelCase` and `PascalCase`.
- Prefer path aliases: `@app/*`, `@server/*`, `@db/*`, `@shared/*`, `@types/*`.
- For Effect reference and usage patterns, consult the local Effect source at `/Users/hedde/code/effect` when behavior or recommended composition is unclear.
- Run `bun run lint:fix` before `bun run lint:check`.

## Testing Guidelines

- Keep unit tests deterministic and close to source files.
- Use Playwright tests in `tests/**` as the active e2e suite.

## Commit & Pull Request Guidelines

- Commit messages: imperative mood, concise scope.
- PRs: include purpose, scope, schema/runtime impact, and UI evidence where relevant.
- For releases, add a Knope change file in `.changeset/*.md`.

## Git Workflow

- Use Git Town commands for branch management and shipping.
- For substantial work in a new worktree created from `main`, immediately run `git town hack <branch-name>` in that worktree before making commits.
- For large multi-phase work, keep an assembly branch plus one reviewable child branch per phase or slice.
- Create stacked child branches with `git town append <branch-name>` from the current assembly branch or stack tip.
- Run `git town sync --stack` before starting work and again before proposing review so parent branches stay current.
- Open PRs with `git town propose` so each branch targets its parent branch in the stack instead of manually selecting a base.

## Security & Configuration

- Never commit secrets.
- In CI, do not rely on generated env artifacts. Use checked-in baseline env files such as `.env.ci` where applicable, and provide CI-specific configuration via explicit environment variables.

## Agent Editing Workflow

- Before calling WebStorm `get_file_problems` on edited files, run `bun run lint:fix` first.
- After editing a file, run WebStorm `get_file_problems` on that file when possible before finishing.
- WebStorm MCP tools are available; prefer sequential `get_file_problems` checks (parallel runs can timeout).


## JavaScript REPL (Node)
- Use `js_repl` for Node-backed JavaScript with top-level await in a persistent kernel.
- `js_repl` is a freeform/custom tool. Direct `js_repl` calls must send raw JavaScript tool input (optionally with first-line `// codex-js-repl: timeout_ms=15000`). Do not wrap code in JSON (for example `{"code":"..."}`), quotes, or markdown code fences.

- `codex.tool` executes a normal tool call and resolves to the raw tool output object. Use it for shell and non-shell tools alike. Nested tool outputs stay inside JavaScript unless you emit them explicitly.
- `codex.emitImage(...)` adds one image to the outer `js_repl` function output each time you call it, so you can call it multiple times to emit multiple images. It accepts a data URL, a single `input_image` item, an object like `{ bytes, mimeType }`, or a raw tool response object with exactly one image and no text. It rejects mixed text-and-image content.
- `codex.tool(...)` and `codex.emitImage(...)` keep stable helper identities across cells. Saved references and persisted objects can reuse them in later cells, but async callbacks that fire after a cell finishes still fail because no exec is active.
- Request full-resolution image processing with `detail: "original"` only when the `view_image` tool schema includes a `detail` argument. The same availability applies to `codex.emitImage(...)`: if `view_image.detail` is present, you may also pass `detail: "original"` there. Use this when high-fidelity image perception or precise localization is needed, especially for CUA agents.
- Example of sharing an in-memory Playwright screenshot: `await codex.emitImage({ bytes: await page.screenshot({ type: "jpeg", quality: 85 }), mimeType: "image/jpeg", detail: "original" })`.
- Example of sharing a local image tool result: `await codex.emitImage(codex.tool("view_image", { path: "/absolute/path", detail: "original" }))`.
- When encoding an image to send with `codex.emitImage(...)` or `view_image`, prefer JPEG at about 85 quality when lossy compression is acceptable; use PNG when transparency or lossless detail matters. Smaller uploads are faster and less likely to hit size limits.
- Top-level bindings persist across cells. If a cell throws, prior bindings remain available and bindings that finished initializing before the throw often remain usable in later cells. For code you plan to reuse across cells, prefer declaring or assigning it in direct top-level statements before operations that might throw. If you hit `SyntaxError: Identifier 'x' has already been declared`, first reuse the existing binding, reassign a previously declared `let`, or pick a new descriptive name. Use `{ ... }` only for a short temporary block when you specifically need local scratch names; do not wrap an entire cell in block scope if you want those names reusable later. Reset the kernel with `js_repl_reset` only when you need a clean state.
- Top-level static import declarations (for example `import x from "./file.js"`) are currently unsupported in `js_repl`; use dynamic imports with `await import("pkg")`, `await import("./file.js")`, or `await import("/abs/path/file.mjs")` instead. <app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Workspace Dependencies
- For sheets, slides, and documents, call `load_workspace_dependencies` to find the bundled runtime and libraries.
- If workspace dependencies are missing, call `install_workspace_dependencies` first.

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use the automation tool
- Automations can wake you up later with a recurring prompt. Use `automation_update` when a future wakeup is useful, especially when the user asks you to monitor, wait, remind them, follow up, or keep working later.
- Delete an automation when its purpose has been fulfilled or it is no longer useful. Be especially willing to delete thread heartbeats that are watching bounded work after that work completes, fails, is canceled, or is replaced by something else.
- Use good judgment before changing or deleting automations that are not clearly tied to the current thread or task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and call `automation_update` with mode="view" for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using `automation_update` (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Tool arguments
- Modes: view, create, update, delete, suggested_update, suggested_create. View, update, delete, and suggested_update MUST include id; create and suggested_create must omit id.
- For view calls, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For create/update/suggested_update/suggested_create, include name, prompt, rrule, kind, and status.
- For delete calls, include only id and mode.
- For cron automations, include cwds, executionEnvironment, model, and reasoningEffort. cwds can be a JSON array or comma-separated string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces or thread it should use (if any), then map those answers into the tool call arguments.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Cron scheduling constraints
- These RRULE limitations apply to cron automations (to match the current UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI. Heartbeat automations attached to a thread can also use minute-based intervals or daily/weekly wall-clock schedules; see the thread automation guidance below.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Choosing an automation style
- There are two main automation paradigms:
  - Cron automations run as separate recurring jobs against one or more workspaces.
  - Thread automations create a heartbeat attached to the current local thread. A heartbeat is a scheduled message that wakes you up in this conversation on an interval or wall-clock schedule.
- Prefer a thread automation in most cases. Heartbeats continue in the same thread, which keeps the work easier to follow and lets future runs build on the existing conversation context. When unsure, choose a thread automation.
- Use a cron automation when the user clearly wants the recurring work to run in a separate conversation instead of continuing in this thread. This is a less common use case, but some users may prefer it.
- If the user asks for a cadence below one hour, consider a thread automation. Cron automations do not support sub-hour intervals in the current UI.
- If the user explicitly asks to do something in this thread every 30 minutes or on another sub-hour interval, that is a valid thread heartbeat request. Do not convert it to an hourly cron automation just because cron automations are limited to hourly or weekly schedules.

#### Thread automation additions
- For heartbeat automations attached to the current local thread, use `kind="heartbeat"` together with `destination="thread"`.
- Do not invent or include raw thread ids for the current thread. The app will resolve `destination="thread"` to the active local thread automatically.
- For thread automations, describe the durable thing to do when the thread wakes up. Keep the prompt short enough to revisit and update as the task changes.
- Thread automations can use minute-based intervals such as every 30 minutes or wall-clock schedules such as weekdays at 9 AM.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- Pull request instructions: Pull request titles should follow conventional commit style.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

{
  "name": "evorto",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "scripts": {
    "env:runtime": "bun helpers/testing/runtime-env.ts",
    "dev:ng": "bunx --bun ng",
    "dev:start": "bunx --bun ng serve",
    "build:app": "NG_BUILD_PARTIAL_SSR=1 bunx --bun ng build",
    "build:watch": "NG_BUILD_PARTIAL_SSR=1 bunx --bun ng build --watch --configuration development",
    "test:unit": "bunx --bun ng test",
    "test:unit:server": "bunx vitest run --config vitest.config.ts",
    "test:unit:server:watch": "bunx vitest --config vitest.config.ts",
    "lint:check": "bunx --bun ng lint",
    "lint:fix": "bunx --bun ng lint --fix",
    "format:write": "prettier --write .",
    "test:e2e": "sh -c 'E2E_MODE=baseline dotenv -c dev -- playwright test --project=local-chrome-baseline \"$@\"' --",
    "test:e2e:ui": "sh -c 'E2E_MODE=baseline dotenv -c dev -- playwright test --project=setup && E2E_MODE=baseline dotenv -c dev -- playwright test --project=local-chrome-baseline --ui \"$@\"' --",
    "test:e2e:docs": "sh -c 'E2E_MODE=baseline dotenv -c dev -- playwright test --project=docs-baseline \"$@\"' --",
    "test:e2e:report": "playwright show-report",
    "db:push": "sh -c 'dotenv -c dev -- drizzle-kit push --force \"$@\"' --",
    "db:studio": "sh -c 'dotenv -c dev -- drizzle-kit studio \"$@\"' --",
    "db:setup": "sh -c 'dotenv -c dev -- drizzle-kit push --force && dotenv -c dev -- bun helpers/database.ts'",
    "db:reset": "bun run db:setup",
    "docker:stop": "sh -c 'dotenv -c dev -- docker compose --env-file /dev/null down'",
    "docker:start": "sh -c 'dotenv -c dev -- docker compose --env-file /dev/null down && dotenv -c dev -- docker compose --env-file /dev/null up --build -d'",
    "docker:start:watch": "sh -c 'dotenv -c dev -- docker compose --env-file /dev/null down && dotenv -c dev -- docker compose --env-file /dev/null up --build -w'",
    "docker:start:foreground": "sh -c 'dotenv -c dev -- docker compose --env-file /dev/null down && dotenv -c dev -- docker compose --env-file /dev/null up --build'",
    "db:migrate": "bun migration/index.ts",
    "deps:update:angular": "bunx --bun ng update @angular/cli @angular/cdk @angular/core @angular/material angular-eslint --allow-dirty",
    "deps:update:drizzle": "bun add drizzle-orm@beta && bun add -D drizzle-seed@beta drizzle-kit@beta",
    "ops:stripe:listen": "stripe listen --forward-to http://localhost:4200/webhooks/stripe",
    "ops:sentry:sourcemaps": "sentry-cli sourcemaps inject --org lukas-heddendorp --project evorto ./dist && sentry-cli sourcemaps upload --org lukas-heddendorp --project evorto ./dist",
    "ui:theme:generate": "bunx --bun ng generate @angular/material:theme-color --include-high-contrast --primary-color=#0891b2 --interactive=false --directory=src/ --force",
    "ui:theme:generate:esn": "bunx --bun ng generate @angular/material:theme-color --include-high-contrast --primary-color=#00aeef --secondary-color=#ec008c --tertiary-color=#7ac143 --interactive=false --directory=src/_esn --force",
    "test:cleanup:receipt-images": "bun helpers/cleanup-testing-receipt-images.ts",
    "test:cleanup:receipt-images:dry-run": "bun helpers/cleanup-testing-receipt-images.ts --dry-run"

services:
  db:
    image: neondatabase/neon_local:v1.5
    environment:
      BRANCH_ID:
      DELETE_BRANCH: "${DELETE_BRANCH:-true}"
      NEON_API_KEY:
      NEON_DATABASE_NAME: "${NEON_DATABASE_NAME:-appdb}"
      NEON_PROJECT_ID:
      PARENT_BRANCH_ID:
    volumes:
      - "${NEON_LOCAL_METADATA_DIR:-./.neon_local}:/tmp/.neon_local"
    ports:
      - "${NEON_LOCAL_HOST_PORT:-55432}:5432"
    healthcheck:
      test:
        [
          "CMD-SHELL",
          "pg_isready -h 127.0.0.1 -p 5432 -U neon -d ${NEON_DATABASE_NAME:-appdb}",
        ]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 20
      start_period: 10s

  db-setup:
    build:
      context: .
      target: build
      args:
        FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN: "${FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN:-}"
      secrets:
        - FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://neon:npg@db:5432/${NEON_DATABASE_NAME:-appdb}?sslmode=require
      NEON_LOCAL_PROXY: "true"
    command:
      - /bin/sh
      - -lc
      - bun ./node_modules/drizzle-kit/bin.cjs push --force && bun helpers/database.ts
    restart: "no"

  minio:
    image: minio/minio:latest
    command: server /data --console-address ":9001"
    environment:
      MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD: "${MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD:-minioadmin}"
      MINIO_ROOT_USER: "${MINIO_ROOT_USER:-minioadmin}"
    ports:
      - "${MINIO_HOST_PORT:-9000}:9000"
      - "${MINIO_CONSOLE_HOST_PORT:-9001}:9001"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://127.0.0.1:9000/minio/health/live"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 20
      start_period: 10s

  minio-init:
    image: minio/mc:latest
    depends_on:
      minio:
        condition: service_healthy
    environment:
      MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD: "${MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD:-minioadmin}"
      MINIO_ROOT_USER: "${MINIO_ROOT_USER:-minioadmin}"
      S3_BUCKET: "${S3_BUCKET:-evorto-testing}"
    entrypoint: /bin/sh
    command:
      - -c
      - |
        mc alias set local http://minio:9000 "$${MINIO_ROOT_USER}" "$${MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD}"
        mc mb --ignore-existing "local/$${S3_BUCKET}"
    restart: "no"

  evorto:
    build:
      context: .
      args:
        FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN: "${FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN:-}"
      secrets:
        - FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
      db-setup:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      minio:
        condition: service_healthy
      minio-init:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
    develop:
      watch:
        - action: rebuild
          path: ./src
        - action: rebuild
          path: package.json
    environment:
      BASE_URL: http://localhost:${APP_HOST_PORT:-4200}
      CLIENT_ID:
      CLIENT_SECRET:
      CLOUDFLARE_IMAGES_API_TOKEN:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://neon:npg@db:5432/${NEON_DATABASE_NAME:-appdb}?sslmode=require
      NEON_LOCAL_PROXY: "true"
      PORT: "4200"
      S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID: "${S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID:-minioadmin}"
      S3_BUCKET: "${S3_BUCKET:-evorto-testing}"
      S3_ENDPOINT: "${S3_ENDPOINT:-http://minio:9000}"
      S3_REGION: "${S3_REGION:-us-east-1}"
      S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: "${S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY:-minioadmin}"
      STRIPE_API_KEY:
      STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET:
    command:
      - /bin/sh
      - -lc
      - mkdir -p /app/logs && bun --preload ./instrument.mjs dist/evorto/server/server.mjs 2>&1 | tee -a /app/logs/server.log
    ports:
      - "${APP_HOST_PORT:-4200}:4200"

  stripe:
    image: stripe/stripe-cli:v1.25.0
    restart: always
    environment:
      STRIPE_API_KEY:
      STRIPE_DEVICE_NAME: integration-tests
    command: "listen --forward-to http://evorto:4200/webhooks/stripe"

secrets:
  FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN:
    environment: FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN

payload.aggregated_output:
name: E2E Baseline

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
  pull_request:

concurrency:
  group: e2e-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  e2e:
    name: Playwright E2E (functional + docs)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    env:
      FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN }}
      CI: true
      E2E_MODE: baseline
      APP_HOST_PORT: 4200
      BASE_URL: http://localhost:4200
      APP_READY_PATH: /robots.txt
      COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME: evorto-ci
      DELETE_BRANCH: true
      NEON_DATABASE_NAME: appdb
      NEON_LOCAL_HOST_PORT: 55432
      NEON_LOCAL_METADATA_DIR: /tmp/neon-local-metadata
      NEON_LOCAL_PROXY: true
      NO_WEBSERVER: true
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://neon:npg@localhost:55432/appdb?sslmode=require
      NEON_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.NEON_API_KEY }}
      NEON_PROJECT_ID: ${{ vars.NEON_PROJECT_ID }}
      PARENT_BRANCH_ID: ${{ secrets.PARENT_BRANCH_ID }}
      CLIENT_ID: ${{ secrets.CLIENT_ID }}
      CLIENT_SECRET: ${{ secrets.CLIENT_SECRET }}
      STRIPE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.STRIPE_API_KEY }}
      STRIPE_TEST_ACCOUNT_ID: ${{ secrets.STRIPE_TEST_ACCOUNT_ID || vars.STRIPE_TEST_ACCOUNT_ID }}
      STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET: ${{ secrets.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET }}
      S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID: minioadmin
      S3_BUCKET: evorto-ci
      S3_ENDPOINT: http://minio:9000
      S3_REGION: us-east-1
      S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: minioadmin
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Bun
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: "1.3.7"

      - name: Validate required configuration
        run: |
          if [ -z "${FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN}" ]; then
            echo "::error::Missing required secret: FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN"
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ -z "${NEON_API_KEY}" ]; then
            echo "::error::Missing required secret: NEON_API_KEY"
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ -z "${NEON_PROJECT_ID}" ]; then
            echo "::error::Missing required repository variable: NEON_PROJECT_ID"
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ -z "${STRIPE_TEST_ACCOUNT_ID}" ]; then
            echo "::error::Missing required Stripe connected account id. Set STRIPE_TEST_ACCOUNT_ID as a secret or repository variable."
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ -z "${CLIENT_SECRET}" ]; then
            echo "::error::Missing required secret: CLIENT_SECRET"
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ -z "${STRIPE_API_KEY}" ]; then
            echo "::error::Missing required secret: STRIPE_API_KEY"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Install Playwright browsers
        run: bunx playwright install --with-deps

      - name: Prepare Neon Local metadata directory
        run: |
          rm -rf "${NEON_LOCAL_METADATA_DIR}"
          mkdir -p "${NEON_LOCAL_METADATA_DIR}"
          chmod 0755 "${NEON_LOCAL_METADATA_DIR}"

      - name: Pull Docker images (compose)
        run: docker compose --env-file /dev/null pull

      - name: Build Docker images (compose)
        run: docker compose --env-file /dev/null build --pull

      - name: Start Docker stack for E2E
        run: |
          docker compose --env-file /dev/null down --remove-orphans
          docker compose --env-file /dev/null up -d db minio stripe minio-init
          for attempt in $(seq 1 60); do
            if docker compose --env-file /dev/null exec -T db pg_isready -h 127.0.0.1 -p 5432 -U neon -d "${NEON_DATABASE_NAME}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "Database is reachable"
              exit 0
            fi
            sleep 2
          done
          echo "::error::Database did not become reachable within 120 seconds"
          docker compose --env-file /dev/null ps
          docker compose --env-file /dev/null logs --no-color --tail=100 db evorto
          exit 1

      - name: Apply latest schema
        run: bun run db:push

      - name: Start application container
        run: docker compose --env-file /dev/null up -d evorto

      - name: Wait for application
        run: |
          ready_url="${BASE_URL}${APP_READY_PATH}"
          for attempt in $(seq 1 60); do
            if curl --fail --silent --show-error "${ready_url}" >/dev/null; then
              echo "Application is reachable at ${ready_url}"
              exit 0
            fi
            sleep 2
          done
          echo "::error::Application did not become reachable at ${ready_url} within 120 seconds"
          docker compose --env-file /dev/null ps
          docker compose --env-file /dev/null logs --no-color --tail=100
          exit 1

      - name: Run Playwright suites
        run: |
          mkdir -p test-results/docker-logs
          if [ "${ACTIONS_STEP_DEBUG:-false}" = "true" ]; then
            docker compose --env-file /dev/null logs -f --no-color 2>&1 | tee test-results/docker-logs/live-docker.log &
            LOG_STREAM_PID=$!
            cleanup() {
              if [ -n "${LOG_STREAM_PID:-}" ] && kill -0 "${LOG_STREAM_PID}" 2>/dev/null; then
                kill "${LOG_STREAM_PID}" || true
                wait "${LOG_STREAM_PID}" || true
              fi
            }
            trap cleanup EXIT
          fi

          bun run test:e2e

          DOCS_OUT_DIR=test-results/docs \
          DOCS_IMG_OUT_DIR=test-results/docs/images \
          bunx playwright test --project=docs-baseline --grep-invert "@finance" \
            --reporter=github,dot,./tests/support/reporters/documentation-reporter.ts

      - name: Collect Docker logs
        if: always()
        run: |
          mkdir -p test-results/docker-logs
          docker compose --env-file /dev/null logs --no-color > test-results/docker-logs/docker-compose.log || true
          evorto_container_id="$(docker compose --env-file /dev/null ps -q evorto)"
          if [ -n "${evorto_container_id}" ]; then
            docker cp "${evorto_container_id}:/app/logs/server.log" test-results/docker-logs/server.log || true
          fi

      - name: Stop Docker stack
        if: always()
        run: docker compose --env-file /dev/null down --remove-orphans

      - name: Upload Playwright test results
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: playwright-test-results
          path: |
            test-results/**
            playwright-report/**
          if-no-files-found: ignore
          retention-days: 7

      - name: Upload generated docs
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: e2e-docs
          path: test-results/docs/**
          if-no-files-found: ignore
          retention-days: 14

name: E2E Baseline

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
  pull_request:

concurrency:
  group: e2e-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  e2e:
    name: Playwright E2E (functional + docs)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    env:
      FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN }}
      CI: true
      E2E_MODE: baseline
      APP_HOST_PORT: 4200
      BASE_URL: http://localhost:4200
      APP_READY_PATH: /robots.txt
      COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME: evorto-ci
      DELETE_BRANCH: true
      NEON_DATABASE_NAME: appdb
      NEON_LOCAL_HOST_PORT: 55432
      NEON_LOCAL_METADATA_DIR: /tmp/neon-local-metadata
      NEON_LOCAL_PROXY: true
      NO_WEBSERVER: true
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://neon:npg@localhost:55432/appdb?sslmode=require
      NEON_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.NEON_API_KEY }}
      NEON_PROJECT_ID: ${{ vars.NEON_PROJECT_ID }}
      PARENT_BRANCH_ID: ${{ secrets.PARENT_BRANCH_ID }}
      CLIENT_ID: ${{ secrets.CLIENT_ID }}
      CLIENT_SECRET: ${{ secrets.CLIENT_SECRET }}
      STRIPE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.STRIPE_API_KEY }}
      STRIPE_TEST_ACCOUNT_ID: ${{ secrets.STRIPE_TEST_ACCOUNT_ID || vars.STRIPE_TEST_ACCOUNT_ID }}
      STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET: ${{ secrets.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET }}
      S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID: minioadmin
      S3_BUCKET: evorto-ci
      S3_ENDPOINT: http://minio:9000
      S3_REGION: us-east-1
      S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: minioadmin
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Bun
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: "1.3.7"

      - name: Validate required configuration
        run: |
          if [ -z "${FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN}" ]; then
            echo "::error::Missing required secret: FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN"
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ -z "${NEON_API_KEY}" ]; then
            echo "::error::Missing required secret: NEON_API_KEY"
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ -z "${NEON_PROJECT_ID}" ]; then
            echo "::error::Missing required repository variable: NEON_PROJECT_ID"
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ -z "${STRIPE_TEST_ACCOUNT_ID}" ]; then
            echo "::error::Missing required Stripe connected account id. Set STRIPE_TEST_ACCOUNT_ID as a secret or repository variable."
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ -z "${CLIENT_SECRET}" ]; then
            echo "::error::Missing required secret: CLIENT_SECRET"
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ -z "${STRIPE_API_KEY}" ]; then
            echo "::error::Missing required secret: STRIPE_API_KEY"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Install Playwright browsers
        run: bunx playwright install --with-deps

      - name: Prepare Neon Local metadata directory
        run: |
          rm -rf "${NEON_LOCAL_METADATA_DIR}"
          mkdir -p "${NEON_LOCAL_METADATA_DIR}"
          chmod 0755 "${NEON_LOCAL_METADATA_DIR}"

      - name: Pull Docker images (compose)
        run: docker compose --env-file /dev/null pull

      - name: Build Docker images (compose)
        run: docker compose --env-file /dev/null build --pull

      - name: Start Docker stack for E2E
        run: |
          docker compose --env-file /dev/null down --remove-orphans
          docker compose --env-file /dev/null up -d db minio stripe minio-init
          for attempt in $(seq 1 60); do
            if docker compose --env-file /dev/null exec -T db pg_isready -h 127.0.0.1 -p 5432 -U neon -d "${NEON_DATABASE_NAME}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "Database is reachable"
              exit 0
            fi
            sleep 2
          done
          echo "::error::Database did not become reachable within 120 seconds"
          docker compose --env-file /dev/null ps
          docker compose --env-file /dev/null logs --no-color --tail=100 db evorto
          exit 1

      - name: Apply latest schema
        run: bun run db:push

      - name: Start application container
        run: docker compose --env-file /dev/null up -d evorto

      - name: Wait for application
        run: |
          ready_url="${BASE_URL}${APP_READY_PATH}"
          for attempt in $(seq 1 60); do
            if curl --fail --silent --show-error "${ready_url}" >/dev/null; then
              echo "Application is reachable at ${ready_url}"
              exit 0
            fi
            sleep 2
          done
          echo "::error::Application did not become reachable at ${ready_url} within 120 seconds"
          docker compose --env-file /dev/null ps
          docker compose --env-file /dev/null logs --no-color --tail=100
          exit 1

      - name: Run Playwright suites
        run: |
          mkdir -p test-results/docker-logs
          if [ "${ACTIONS_STEP_DEBUG:-false}" = "true" ]; then
            docker compose --env-file /dev/null logs -f --no-color 2>&1 | tee test-results/docker-logs/live-docker.log &
            LOG_STREAM_PID=$!
            cleanup() {
              if [ -n "${LOG_STREAM_PID:-}" ] && kill -0 "${LOG_STREAM_PID}" 2>/dev/null; then
                kill "${LOG_STREAM_PID}" || true
                wait "${LOG_STREAM_PID}" || true
              fi
            }
            trap cleanup EXIT
          fi

          bun run test:e2e

          DOCS_OUT_DIR=test-results/docs \
          DOCS_IMG_OUT_DIR=test-results/docs/images \
          bunx playwright test --project=docs-baseline --grep-invert "@finance" \
            --reporter=github,dot,./tests/support/reporters/documentation-reporter.ts

      - name: Collect Docker logs
        if: always()
        run: |
          mkdir -p test-results/docker-logs
          docker compose --env-file /dev/null logs --no-color > test-results/docker-logs/docker-compose.log || true
          evorto_container_id="$(docker compose --env-file /dev/null ps -q evorto)"
          if [ -n "${evorto_container_id}" ]; then
            docker cp "${evorto_container_id}:/app/logs/server.log" test-results/docker-logs/server.log || true
          fi

      - name: Stop Docker stack
        if: always()
        run: docker compose --env-file /dev/null down --remove-orphans

      - name: Upload Playwright test results
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: playwright-test-results
          path: |
            test-results/**
            playwright-report/**
          if-no-files-found: ignore
          retention-days: 7

      - name: Upload generated docs
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: e2e-docs
          path: test-results/docs/**
          if-no-files-found: ignore
          retention-days: 14

payload.aggregated_output:
# Database Seeding

This directory contains scripts for setting up and seeding the database with
development, documentation, and Playwright test data.

## Overview

The database seeding process serves two distinct goals:

- `demo` profile: plausible demo/development data for local usage
- `test` profile: deterministic fixtures for isolated Playwright tenants
- `docs` profile: deterministic shared dataset for documentation journeys

## Key Files

- `database.ts`: Main entry point for database setup and seeding
- `seed-tenant.ts`: Shared tenant seeding logic used by tests, development, and demos
- `add-events.ts`: Creates events with deterministic dates, statuses, and visibilities
- `add-roles.ts`: Sets up user roles and permissions
- `add-templates.ts`: Creates event templates
- `add-template-categories.ts`: Sets up template categories
- `user-data.ts`: Defines test users
- `seed-clock.ts`: Resolves deterministic seeded time
- `seed-falso.ts`: Resolves deterministic pseudo-random seed key

## Seeding Approach

The seeding approach is deterministic, but not every profile has the same goal:

1. **Profiles**
   - `demo` keeps the richer, more realistic local dataset.
   - `test` and `docs` expose stable scenario handles instead of relying on fuzzy discovery.

2. **Scenario Contract**
   - `seedTenant()` returns `result.scenario.events.*` handles.
   - Current scenario handles:
     - `freeOpen`
     - `paidOpen`
     - `closedReg`
     - `past`
     - `draft`
   - Playwright tests should use those handles directly.

3. **Pinned Clock + Seed Key**
   - `seed-clock.ts` honors `E2E_NOW_ISO` when provided.
   - `seed-falso.ts` honors `E2E_SEED_KEY` when provided.
   - Playwright defaults both values in code, so normal test runs do not need extra env wiring.

4. **Deterministic Events**
   - Fixed number of events per template type
   - `demo` keeps the richer local dataset to roughly 50 events and spreads approved, draft, and pending-review states across a more gradual timeline
   - `test` and `docs` keep the smaller stable schedule that backs scenario handles
   - Deterministic assignment of status, visibility, and creator
   - Template selection is based on stable `seedKey` metadata, not title matching

5. **Realistic Data Structure**
   - Events have appropriate registration options
   - Users have appropriate roles and permissions
   - Templates and categories are properly linked

## Running the Seeding Process

To reset and seed the development/demo database:

```bash
bun run db:reset
```

This will:

1. Generate `.env.dev` with `bun run env:runtime` and re-run it whenever local runtime settings change, such as changing local ports or renaming the local project, so Docker and Playwright keep isolated ports/project naming
2. Ensure schema exists and reset/seed the local database (`bun run db:setup`)

`bun run db:setup` now uses the same `dotenv -c dev` loading model as `db:push`. In this repo, the supported local files are `.env` for developer secrets, `.env.dev.local` for tracked shared defaults, and `.env.dev` for generated worktree overrides. `bun run db:push`, Docker's `db-setup` service, and `bun run db:studio` all consume the same local environment contract.

The Neon Local container does not emit every proxied query in its default logging configuration, so `docker logs` staying quiet during `db:reset` does not mean the reset missed Docker.

Docker Compose now also runs a one-shot `db-setup` container before `evorto` starts. That service pushes schema and resets/seeds the Docker database on every stack start, and local Neon branches are configured to be deleted automatically on shutdown. Compose itself does not load repo dotenv files directly; the package scripts preload the needed environment before invoking Docker.

Testing/runtime context that depends on these seed flows lives in [tests/README.md](../tests/README.md).

## Modifying the Seeding Process

If you need to modify the seeding process:

1. Make changes to the appropriate file(s) in the `helpers` directory
2. Test your changes by running `bun run db:reset`
3. Verify that the application displays the expected data

For Playwright tests, prefer consuming `seeded.scenario` in fixtures/specs rather
than searching for events by title, date, or incidental seeded content.

# Playwright Tests

This directory contains the active Playwright suite.

## Structure

- Functional/e2e tests: `tests/specs/**`
- Documentation tests: `tests/docs/**`
- Setup/auth/database bootstrapping lives in `tests/setup/**`
- Shared fixtures/utilities/reporters live in `tests/support/fixtures/**`, `tests/support/utils/**`, `tests/support/reporters/**`

## Fixture Contract

- `tests/support/fixtures/parallel-test.ts` seeds a fresh tenant per test with `profile: 'test'`
- `tests/setup/database.setup.ts` seeds the shared docs tenant with `profile: 'docs'`
- Specs should consume deterministic scenario handles from `seeded.scenario`
- Do not discover test entities by template title fragments, fuzzy event searches, or wall-clock checks

## Required Tags

All tests in `tests/**/*.ts` are linted with a custom ESLint rule:

- `@track(<track_id>)` is required for every test title
- `@req(<id>)` is required for non-doc tests
- `@doc(<id>)` is required for doc tests under `tests/docs/**`

## Commands

```bash
bun run test:e2e
bun run test:e2e:ui
bun run test:e2e:docs
bun run test:e2e -- --project=setup
bun run test:e2e -- --headed --workers 1
bun run test:e2e:docs -- --project=docs-integration
bun run test:e2e:report
bun run lint:check
```

## Docker Runtime

- Generate or refresh worktree-local runtime overrides: `bun run env:runtime`
- Start the local runtime stack: `bun run docker:start`
- Start the local runtime stack in foreground for Playwright `webServer`: `bun run docker:start:foreground`
- Start the local runtime stack in watch mode: `bun run docker:start:watch`
- Stop the local runtime stack: `bun run docker:stop`
- Local Docker runs use Neon Local instead of a plain Postgres container.
- Docker Compose includes a one-shot `db-setup` service that runs the equivalent of `db:setup` before `evorto` starts.
- `bun run env:runtime` generates `.env.dev`, the untracked worktree-local override file.
- `.env.dev.local` is the tracked shared default dev config file.
- `.env` is the untracked developer-secrets file.
- `.env.local`, `.env.runtime`, and `.env.ci` are unsupported in this repo.
- Starting the Docker stack is destructive for local database state by design because `db-setup` pushes schema and resets/seeds the Docker database on every start.
- `bun run test:e2e:ui` pre-runs the Playwright `setup` project before opening UI mode because Playwright UI does not reliably honor the repo's setup dependency chain.
- Docker Compose does not load repo dotenv files directly; local scripts preload the environment with `dotenv -c dev` before invoking Compose.

## Runtime Environment Precedence

Application runtime config resolves in this precedence order:

- real environment variables
- `.env.dev.local`
- `.env.dev`
- `.env`
- in-code defaults

External-tool scripts use `dotenv -c dev`. Because `dotenv-cli` is first-wins, the effective dotenv precedence for those scripts is:

- `.env.dev.local`
- `.env.local` if someone creates it manually; this file is unsupported and should not exist
- `.env.dev`
- `.env`

CI should not rely on dotenv files at all; workflows provide values via exported environment variables.

## Deterministic E2E Environment

Playwright defaults deterministic test values in code via `helpers/testing/deterministic-test-defaults.ts`, so local runs do not need extra flags.

Default values:

- `E2E_NOW_ISO=2026-09-15T12:00:00.000Z`
- `E2E_SEED_KEY=evorto-e2e-default-v1`

Optional overrides:

- `E2E_NOW_ISO`
- `E2E_SEED_KEY`

Keep `E2E_NOW_ISO` ahead of the real current date or deterministic checkout expiry behavior will break.

## Baseline vs Integration Projects

Playwright separates external-service coverage with dedicated projects:

- baseline:
  - `local-chrome-baseline`
  - `docs-baseline`
- integration-only:
  - `docs-integration`

`E2E_MODE=baseline` is the default.
`E2E_MODE=integration` is required when running integration-only projects so runtime validation demands the extra external-service credentials.

Integration-only coverage is tagged at the test-title level:

- `@needs-auth0-management`
- `@needs-cloudflare`
- `@needs-google-maps`

Required for full Playwright flows:

- `DATABASE_URL`
- `BASE_URL`
- `CLIENT_ID`
- `CLIENT_SECRET`

# Database Seeding

This directory contains scripts for setting up and seeding the database with
development, documentation, and Playwright test data.

## Overview

The database seeding process serves two distinct goals:

- `demo` profile: plausible demo/development data for local usage
- `test` profile: deterministic fixtures for isolated Playwright tenants
- `docs` profile: deterministic shared dataset for documentation journeys

## Key Files

- `database.ts`: Main entry point for database setup and seeding
- `seed-tenant.ts`: Shared tenant seeding logic used by tests, development, and demos
- `add-events.ts`: Creates events with deterministic dates, statuses, and visibilities
- `add-roles.ts`: Sets up user roles and permissions
- `add-templates.ts`: Creates event templates
- `add-template-categories.ts`: Sets up template categories
- `user-data.ts`: Defines test users
- `seed-clock.ts`: Resolves deterministic seeded time
- `seed-falso.ts`: Resolves deterministic pseudo-random seed key

## Seeding Approach

The seeding approach is deterministic, but not every profile has the same goal:

1. **Profiles**
   - `demo` keeps the richer, more realistic local dataset.
   - `test` and `docs` expose stable scenario handles instead of relying on fuzzy discovery.

2. **Scenario Contract**
   - `seedTenant()` returns `result.scenario.events.*` handles.
   - Current scenario handles:
     - `freeOpen`
     - `paidOpen`
     - `closedReg`
     - `past`
     - `draft`
   - Playwright tests should use those handles directly.

3. **Pinned Clock + Seed Key**
   - `seed-clock.ts` honors `E2E_NOW_ISO` when provided.
   - `seed-falso.ts` honors `E2E_SEED_KEY` when provided.
   - Playwright defaults both values in code, so normal test runs do not need extra env wiring.

4. **Deterministic Events**
   - Fixed number of events per template type
   - `demo` keeps the richer local dataset to roughly 50 events and spreads approved, draft, and pending-review states across a more gradual timeline
   - `test` and `docs` keep the smaller stable schedule that backs scenario handles
   - Deterministic assignment of status, visibility, and creator
   - Template selection is based on stable `seedKey` metadata, not title matching

5. **Realistic Data Structure**
   - Events have appropriate registration options
   - Users have appropriate roles and permissions
   - Templates and categories are properly linked

## Running the Seeding Process

To reset and seed the development/demo database:

```bash
bun run db:reset
```

This will:

1. Generate `.env.dev` with `bun run env:runtime` and re-run it whenever local runtime settings change, such as changing local ports or renaming the local project, so Docker and Playwright keep isolated ports/project naming
2. Ensure schema exists and reset/seed the local database (`bun run db:setup`)

`bun run db:setup` now uses the same `dotenv -c dev` loading model as `db:push`. In this repo, the supported local files are `.env` for developer secrets, `.env.dev.local` for tracked shared defaults, and `.env.dev` for generated worktree overrides. `bun run db:push`, Docker's `db-setup` service, and `bun run db:studio` all consume the same local environment contract.

The Neon Local container does not emit every proxied query in its default logging configuration, so `docker logs` staying quiet during `db:reset` does not mean the reset missed Docker.

Docker Compose now also runs a one-shot `db-setup` container before `evorto` starts. That service pushes schema and resets/seeds the Docker database on every stack start, and local Neon branches are configured to be deleted automatically on shutdown. Compose itself does not load repo dotenv files directly; the package scripts preload the needed environment before invoking Docker.

Testing/runtime context that depends on these seed flows lives in [tests/README.md](../tests/README.md).

## Modifying the Seeding Process

If you need to modify the seeding process:

1. Make changes to the appropriate file(s) in the `helpers` directory
2. Test your changes by running `bun run db:reset`
3. Verify that the application displays the expected data

For Playwright tests, prefer consuming `seeded.scenario` in fixtures/specs rather
than searching for events by title, date, or incidental seeded content.

1	# Evorto
     2	
     3	This project was generated using [Angular CLI](https://github.com/angular/angular-cli) and is maintained on Angular 21 with Bun-first tooling.
     4	
     5	## Prerequisites
     6	
     7	- Docker Compose 2.24.0 or later is required for the local Docker-backed runtime and E2E flows.
     8	
     9	## Local Environment Files
    10	
    11	This repo uses three local env files:
    12	
    13	- `.env` — untracked developer secrets
    14	- `.env.dev.local` — tracked shared default dev config
    15	- `.env.dev` — generated worktree-specific overrides via `bun run env:runtime`
    16	
    17	`.env.local`, `.env.runtime`, and `.env.ci` are unsupported in this repo.
    18	
    19	## Git workflow
    20	
    21	This repository uses Git Town to manage branching, syncing, and shipping. Prefer `git town` commands for daily workflow.
    22	
    23	For large multi-phase changes, keep an assembly branch plus one child branch per reviewable phase. Create the next branch with `git town append`, keep the stack current with `git town sync --stack`, and open PRs with `git town propose`.
    24	
    25	## Release documentation
    26	
    27	We use Knope for release notes.
    28	
    29	- Always add a change file in `.changeset/*.md` for release-relevant work.
    30	- Do not rely on conventional commits or PR titles as release documentation.
    31	
    32	## Development server
    33	
    34	To start a local development server, run:
    35	
    36	```bash
    37	bun run dev:start
    38	```
    39	
    40	Once the server is running, open your browser and navigate to `http://localhost:4200/`. The application will automatically reload whenever you modify any of the source files.
    41	
    42	## Code scaffolding
    43	
    44	Angular CLI includes powerful code scaffolding tools. To generate a new component, run:
    45	
    46	```bash
    47	bun run dev:ng generate component component-name
    48	```
    49	
    50	For a complete list of available schematics (such as `components`, `directives`, or `pipes`), run:
    51	
    52	```bash
    53	bun run dev:ng generate --help
    54	```
    55	
    56	## Building
    57	
    58	To build the project run:
    59	
    60	```bash

1	services:
     2	  db:
     3	    image: neondatabase/neon_local:v1.5
     4	    environment:
     5	      BRANCH_ID:
     6	      DELETE_BRANCH: "${DELETE_BRANCH:-true}"
     7	      NEON_API_KEY:
     8	      NEON_DATABASE_NAME: "${NEON_DATABASE_NAME:-appdb}"
     9	      NEON_PROJECT_ID:
    10	      PARENT_BRANCH_ID:
    11	    volumes:
    12	      - "${NEON_LOCAL_METADATA_DIR:-./.neon_local}:/tmp/.neon_local"
    13	    ports:
    14	      - "${NEON_LOCAL_HOST_PORT:-55432}:5432"
    15	    healthcheck:
    16	      test:
    17	        [
    18	          "CMD-SHELL",
    19	          "pg_isready -h 127.0.0.1 -p 5432 -U neon -d ${NEON_DATABASE_NAME:-appdb}",
    20	        ]
    21	      interval: 5s
    22	      timeout: 5s
    23	      retries: 20
    24	      start_period: 10s
    25	
    26	  db-setup:
    27	    build:
    28	      context: .
    29	      target: build
    30	      args:
    31	        FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN: "${FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN:-}"
    32	      secrets:
    33	        - FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN
    34	    depends_on:
    35	      db:
    36	        condition: service_healthy
    37	    environment:
    38	      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://neon:npg@db:5432/${NEON_DATABASE_NAME:-appdb}?sslmode=require
    39	      NEON_LOCAL_PROXY: "true"
    40	    command:
    41	      - /bin/sh
    42	      - -lc
    43	      - bun ./node_modules/drizzle-kit/bin.cjs push --force && bun helpers/database.ts
    44	    restart: "no"
    45	
    46	  minio:
    47	    image: minio/minio:latest
    48	    command: server /data --console-address ":9001"
    49	    environment:
    50	      MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD: "${MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD:-minioadmin}"
    51	      MINIO_ROOT_USER: "${MINIO_ROOT_USER:-minioadmin}"
    52	    ports:
    53	      - "${MINIO_HOST_PORT:-9000}:9000"
    54	      - "${MINIO_CONSOLE_HOST_PORT:-9001}:9001"
    55	    healthcheck:
    56	      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://127.0.0.1:9000/minio/health/live"]
    57	      interval: 5s
    58	      timeout: 5s
    59	      retries: 20
    60	      start_period: 10s
    61	
    62	  minio-init:
    63	    image: minio/mc:latest
    64	    depends_on:
    65	      minio:
    66	        condition: service_healthy
    67	    environment:
    68	      MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD: "${MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD:-minioadmin}"
    69	      MINIO_ROOT_USER: "${MINIO_ROOT_USER:-minioadmin}"
    70	      S3_BUCKET: "${S3_BUCKET:-evorto-testing}"
    71	    entrypoint: /bin/sh
    72	    command:
    73	      - -c
    74	      - |
    75	        mc alias set local http://minio:9000 "$${MINIO_ROOT_USER}" "$${MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD}"
    76	        mc mb --ignore-existing "local/$${S3_BUCKET}"
    77	    restart: "no"
    78	
    79	  evorto:
    80	    build:
    81	      context: .
    82	      args:
    83	        FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN: "${FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN:-}"
    84	      secrets:
    85	        - FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN
    86	    depends_on:
    87	      db:
    88	        condition: service_healthy
    89	      db-setup:
    90	        condition: service_completed_successfully
    91	      minio:
    92	        condition: service_healthy
    93	      minio-init:
    94	        condition: service_completed_successfully
    95	    develop:
    96	      watch:
    97	        - action: rebuild
    98	          path: ./src
    99	        - action: rebuild
   100	          path: package.json
   101	    environment:
   102	      BASE_URL: http://localhost:${APP_HOST_PORT:-4200}
   103	      CLIENT_ID:
   104	      CLIENT_SECRET:
   105	      CLOUDFLARE_IMAGES_API_TOKEN:
   106	      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://neon:npg@db:5432/${NEON_DATABASE_NAME:-appdb}?sslmode=require
   107	      NEON_LOCAL_PROXY: "true"
   108	      PORT: "4200"
   109	      S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID: "${S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID:-minioadmin}"
   110	      S3_BUCKET: "${S3_BUCKET:-evorto-testing}"
   111	      S3_ENDPOINT: "${S3_ENDPOINT:-http://minio:9000}"
   112	      S3_REGION: "${S3_REGION:-us-east-1}"
   113	      S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: "${S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY:-minioadmin}"
   114	      STRIPE_API_KEY:
   115	      STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET:
   116	    command:
   117	      - /bin/sh
   118	      - -lc
   119	      - mkdir -p /app/logs && bun --preload ./instrument.mjs dist/evorto/server/server.mjs 2>&1 | tee -a /app/logs/server.log
   120	    ports:
   121	      - "${APP_HOST_PORT:-4200}:4200"
   122	
   123	  stripe:
   124	    image: stripe/stripe-cli:v1.25.0
   125	    restart: always
   126	    environment:
   127	      STRIPE_API_KEY:
   128	      STRIPE_DEVICE_NAME: integration-tests
   129	    command: "listen --forward-to http://evorto:4200/webhooks/stripe"
   130	
   131	secrets:
   132	  FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN:
   133	    environment: FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN

    42	- Generate or refresh worktree-local runtime overrides: `bun run env:runtime`
    43	- Start the local runtime stack: `bun run docker:start`
    44	- Start the local runtime stack in foreground for Playwright `webServer`: `bun run docker:start:foreground`
    45	- Start the local runtime stack in watch mode: `bun run docker:start:watch`
    46	- Stop the local runtime stack: `bun run docker:stop`
    47	- Local Docker runs use Neon Local instead of a plain Postgres container.
    48	- Docker Compose includes a one-shot `db-setup` service that runs the equivalent of `db:setup` before `evorto` starts.
    49	- `bun run env:runtime` generates `.env.dev`, the untracked worktree-local override file.
    50	- `.env.dev.local` is the tracked shared default dev config file.
    51	- `.env` is the untracked developer-secrets file.
    52	- `.env.local`, `.env.runtime`, and `.env.ci` are unsupported in this repo.
    53	- Starting the Docker stack is destructive for local database state by design because `db-setup` pushes schema and resets/seeds the Docker database on every start.
    54	- `bun run test:e2e:ui` pre-runs the Playwright `setup` project before opening UI mode because Playwright UI does not reliably honor the repo's setup dependency chain.
    55	- Docker Compose does not load repo dotenv files directly; local scripts preload the environment with `dotenv -c dev` before invoking Compose.
    56	
    57	## Runtime Environment Precedence
    58	
    59	Application runtime config resolves in this precedence order:
    60	
    61	- real environment variables
    62	- `.env.dev.local`
    63	- `.env.dev`
    64	- `.env`
    65	- in-code defaults
    66	
    67	External-tool scripts use `dotenv -c dev`. Because `dotenv-cli` is first-wins, the effective dotenv precedence for those scripts is:
    68	
    69	- `.env.dev.local`
    70	- `.env.local` if someone creates it manually; this file is unsupported and should not exist
    71	- `.env.dev`
    72	- `.env`
    73	
    74	CI should not rely on dotenv files at all; workflows provide values via exported environment variables.
    75	
    76	## Deterministic E2E Environment
    77	
    78	Playwright defaults deterministic test values in code via `helpers/testing/deterministic-test-defaults.ts`, so local runs do not need extra flags.
    79	
    80	Default values:
    81	
    82	- `E2E_NOW_ISO=2026-09-15T12:00:00.000Z`
    83	- `E2E_SEED_KEY=evorto-e2e-default-v1`
    84	
    85	Optional overrides:

payload.aggregated_output:
1	name: E2E Baseline
     2	
     3	on:
     4	  workflow_dispatch:
     5	  push:
     6	    branches: [main, develop]
     7	  pull_request:
     8	
     9	concurrency:
    10	  group: e2e-${{ github.ref }}
    11	  cancel-in-progress: true
    12	
    13	jobs:
    14	  e2e:
    15	    name: Playwright E2E (functional + docs)
    16	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    17	    timeout-minutes: 60
    18	    env:
    19	      FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN }}
    20	      CI: true
    21	      E2E_MODE: baseline
    22	      APP_HOST_PORT: 4200
    23	      BASE_URL: http://localhost:4200
    24	      APP_READY_PATH: /robots.txt
    25	      COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME: evorto-ci
    26	      DELETE_BRANCH: true
    27	      NEON_DATABASE_NAME: appdb
    28	      NEON_LOCAL_HOST_PORT: 55432
    29	      NEON_LOCAL_METADATA_DIR: /tmp/neon-local-metadata
    30	      NEON_LOCAL_PROXY: true
    31	      NO_WEBSERVER: true
    32	      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://neon:npg@localhost:55432/appdb?sslmode=require
    33	      NEON_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.NEON_API_KEY }}
    34	      NEON_PROJECT_ID: ${{ vars.NEON_PROJECT_ID }}
    35	      PARENT_BRANCH_ID: ${{ secrets.PARENT_BRANCH_ID }}
    36	      CLIENT_ID: ${{ secrets.CLIENT_ID }}
    37	      CLIENT_SECRET: ${{ secrets.CLIENT_SECRET }}
    38	      STRIPE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.STRIPE_API_KEY }}
    39	      STRIPE_TEST_ACCOUNT_ID: ${{ secrets.STRIPE_TEST_ACCOUNT_ID || vars.STRIPE_TEST_ACCOUNT_ID }}
    40	      STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET: ${{ secrets.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET }}
    41	      S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID: minioadmin
    42	      S3_BUCKET: evorto-ci
    43	      S3_ENDPOINT: http://minio:9000
    44	      S3_REGION: us-east-1
    45	      S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: minioadmin
    46	    steps:
    47	      - name: Checkout repository
    48	        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    49	
    50	      - name: Setup Bun
    51	        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
    52	        with:
    53	          bun-version: "1.3.7"
    54	
    55	      - name: Validate required configuration
    56	        run: |
    57	          if [ -z "${FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN}" ]; then
    58	            echo "::error::Missing required secret: FONT_AWESOME_TOKEN"
    59	            exit 1
    60	          fi
    61	          if [ -z "${NEON_API_KEY}" ]; then
    62	            echo "::error::Missing required secret: NEON_API_KEY"
    63	            exit 1
    64	          fi
    65	          if [ -z "${NEON_PROJECT_ID}" ]; then
    66	            echo "::error::Missing required repository variable: NEON_PROJECT_ID"
    67	            exit 1
    68	          fi
    69	          if [ -z "${STRIPE_TEST_ACCOUNT_ID}" ]; then
    70	            echo "::error::Missing required Stripe connected account id. Set STRIPE_TEST_ACCOUNT_ID as a secret or repository variable."
    71	            exit 1
    72	          fi
    73	          if [ -z "${CLIENT_SECRET}" ]; then
    74	            echo "::error::Missing required secret: CLIENT_SECRET"
    75	            exit 1
    76	          fi
    77	          if [ -z "${STRIPE_API_KEY}" ]; then
    78	            echo "::error::Missing required secret: STRIPE_API_KEY"
    79	            exit 1
    80	          fi
    81	
    82	      - name: Install dependencies
    83	        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
    84	
    85	      - name: Install Playwright browsers
    86	        run: bunx playwright install --with-deps
    87	
    88	      - name: Prepare Neon Local metadata directory
    89	        run: |
    90	          rm -rf "${NEON_LOCAL_METADATA_DIR}"
    91	          mkdir -p "${NEON_LOCAL_METADATA_DIR}"
    92	          chmod 0755 "${NEON_LOCAL_METADATA_DIR}"
    93	
    94	      - name: Pull Docker images (compose)
    95	        run: docker compose --env-file /dev/null pull
    96	
    97	      - name: Build Docker images (compose)
    98	        run: docker compose --env-file /dev/null build --pull
    99	
   100	      - name: Start Docker stack for E2E
   101	        run: |
   102	          docker compose --env-file /dev/null down --remove-orphans
   103	          docker compose --env-file /dev/null up -d db minio stripe minio-init
   104	          for attempt in $(seq 1 60); do
   105	            if docker compose --env-file /dev/null exec -T db pg_isready -h 127.0.0.1 -p 5432 -U neon -d "${NEON_DATABASE_NAME}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   106	              echo "Database is reachable"
   107	              exit 0
   108	            fi
   109	            sleep 2
   110	          done
   111	          echo "::error::Database did not become reachable within 120 seconds"
   112	          docker compose --env-file /dev/null ps
   113	          docker compose --env-file /dev/null logs --no-color --tail=100 db evorto
   114	          exit 1
   115	
   116	      - name: Apply latest schema
   117	        run: bun run db:push
   118	
   119	      - name: Start application container
   120	        run: docker compose --env-file /dev/null up -d evorto
   121	
   122	      - name: Wait for application
   123	        run: |
   124	          ready_url="${BASE_URL}${APP_READY_PATH}"
   125	          for attempt in $(seq 1 60); do
   126	            if curl --fail --silent --show-error "${ready_url}" >/dev/null; then
   127	              echo "Application is reachable at ${ready_url}"
   128	              exit 0
   129	            fi
   130	            sleep 2
   131	          done
   132	          echo "::error::Application did not become reachable at ${ready_url} within 120 seconds"
   133	          docker compose --env-file /dev/null ps
   134	          docker compose --env-file /dev/null logs --no-color --tail=100
   135	          exit 1
   136	
   137	      - name: Run Playwright suites
   138	        run: |
   139	          mkdir -p test-results/docker-logs
   140	          if [ "${ACTIONS_STEP_DEBUG:-false}" = "true" ]; then
   141	            docker compose --env-file /dev/null logs -f --no-color 2>&1 | tee test-results/docker-logs/live-docker.log &
   142	            LOG_STREAM_PID=$!
   143	            cleanup() {
   144	              if [ -n "${LOG_STREAM_PID:-}" ] && kill -0 "${LOG_STREAM_PID}" 2>/dev/null; then
   145	                kill "${LOG_STREAM_PID}" || true
   146	                wait "${LOG_STREAM_PID}" || true
   147	              fi
   148	            }
   149	            trap cleanup EXIT
   150	          fi
   151	
   152	          bun run test:e2e
   153	
   154	          DOCS_OUT_DIR=test-results/docs \
   155	          DOCS_IMG_OUT_DIR=test-results/docs/images \
   156	          bunx playwright test --project=docs-baseline --grep-invert "@finance" \
   157	            --reporter=github,dot,./tests/support/reporters/documentation-reporter.ts
   158	
   159	      - name: Collect Docker logs
   160	        if: always()
   161	        run: |
   162	          mkdir -p test-results/docker-logs
   163	          docker compose --env-file /dev/null logs --no-color > test-results/docker-logs/docker-compose.log || true
   164	          evorto_container_id="$(docker compose --env-file /dev/null ps -q evorto)"
   165	          if [ -n "${evorto_container_id}" ]; then
   166	            docker cp "${evorto_container_id}:/app/logs/server.log" test-results/docker-logs/server.log || true
   167	          fi
   168	
   169	      - name: Stop Docker stack
   170	        if: always()
   171	        run: docker compose --env-file /dev/null down --remove-orphans
   172	
   173	      - name: Upload Playwright test results
   174	        if: always()
   175	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
   176	        with:
   177	          name: playwright-test-results
   178	          path: |
   179	            test-results/**
   180	            playwright-report/**

**Changes**
The env model is now centered on `dotenv -c dev` with the repo-specific file roles you asked for. The shared tracked defaults live in [.env.dev.local](/Users/hedde/code/evorto/.env.dev.local:1), the generated worktree override is now written to `.env.dev` by [helpers/testing/runtime-environment.ts](/Users/hedde/code/evorto/helpers/testing/runtime-environment.ts:9), and the runtime config provider now resolves local files in the same repo contract order at [src/server/config/provider.ts](/Users/hedde/code/evorto/src/server/config/provider.ts:13). The provider spec was updated to lock that in at [src/server/config/provider.spec.ts](/Users/hedde/code/evorto/src/server/config/provider.spec.ts:30).

The important scripts stay visible in [package.json](/Users/hedde/code/evorto/package.json:4), but the noisy explicit dotenv chains are gone. `test:e2e*`, `db:*`, and `docker:*` now use `dotenv -c dev`, and the Docker commands force `docker compose` to ignore implicit `.env` loading with `--env-file /dev/null` at [package.json](/Users/hedde/code/evorto/package.json:16). Compose itself no longer uses `env_file` blocks in [docker-compose.yml](/Users/hedde/code/evorto/docker-compose.yml:1). CI was moved to explicit GitHub Actions env/vars/secrets in [.github/workflows/e2e-baseline.yml](/Users/hedde/code/evorto/.github/workflows/e2e-baseline.yml:18), and the local bootstrap now copies only `.env` and `.env.dev.local` before regenerating `.env.dev` in [.codex/environments/environment.toml](/Users/hedde/code/evorto/.codex/environments/environment.toml:11). I also updated the repo guidance in [README.md](/Users/hedde/code/evorto/README.md:9), [tests/README.md](/Users/hedde/code/evorto/tests/README.md:40), [helpers/README.md](/Users/hedde/code/evorto/helpers/README.md:67), [AGENTS.md](/Users/hedde/code/evorto/AGENTS.md:89), and [src/server/config/AGENTS.md](/Users/hedde/code/evorto/src/server/config/AGENTS.md:5).

**Validation**
I ran `bun run lint:fix`, then WebStorm inspections on [helpers/testing/runtime-environment.ts](/Users/hedde/code/evorto/helpers/testing/runtime-environment.ts:1), [src/server/config/provider.ts](/Users/hedde/code/evorto/src/server/config/provider.ts:1), [src/server/config/provider.spec.ts](/Users/hedde/code/evorto/src/server/config/provider.spec.ts:1), and [package.json](/Users/hedde/code/evorto/package.json:1). The changed TS files are clean. `package.json` still has the same two non-task warnings as before: the generic `bun@1.3.11` package-manager pattern warning and the existing `effect` vulnerability notice.

Smoke tests passed for `bun run env:runtime`, `bun run test:e2e -- --help`, `bun run test:e2e:docs -- --help`, and `bun run db:push -- --help`. `bun run docker:stop` still failed because Docker could not stop the existing unhealthy Neon Local container `evorto-d8fcbd79-db-1` and reported `tried to kill container, but did not receive an exit event`; that looks like daemon/container state, not a script regression.

I left the unrelated deletions already present in the worktree untouched.
